file_name: Cerus Corporation - FIRST AMEND TO SUPPLY AND MANUFACTURING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise expressly provided in the applicable Statement of Work payment to Porex of undisputed fees shall be due [*] days following Cerus' receipt of the invoice submitted by Porex. Payment information, including Tax Identification Number will be included on each invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise expressly provided in the applicable Statement of Work payment to Porex of undisputed fees shall be due [*] days following Cerus' receipt of the invoice submitted by Porex. Remuneration details, including Tax Identification Number will be included on each invoice.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Payment information' to 'Remuneration details' introduces ambiguity. While 'payment' generally refers to monetary compensation, 'remuneration' can encompass other forms of compensation. This creates uncertainty about the exact information required on the invoice. This change contradicts the intent in the document, where payment terms are well defined and thus creates uncertainty whether to use this definition or another
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Section 1.5.3 shall be added to the Agreement as follows: 1.5.3 Porex will invoice Cerus monthly for Services performed by Porex during the prior month. Unless otherwise expressly provided in the applicable Statement of Work payment to Porex of undisputed fees shall be due [*] days following Cerus' receipt of the invoice submitted by Porex. Remuneration details, including Tax Identification Number will be included on each invoice. Cerus shall have the right to accept or reject the Services, or any portion thereof, in writing within [*] days from Porex's completion of the Services. Such acceptance or rejection shall be consistent with the criteria set forth in the Statement of Work, if any. If Cerus does not reject in writing within [*] days, the Services shall be considered accepted by Cerus. Within [*] days of any notice of rejection, Porex shall present a corrective plan of action to Cerus. Upon approval by Cerus of the corrective plan, Porex, at no additional expense to Cerus, shall then make the corrections and, where applicable, Porex shall resubmit the corrected Services to Cerus. For disputed invoices or the disputed portion of an invoice, Cerus shall use reasonable efforts to provide to Porex, in writing, within [*] days, a description of the disputed amounts. Cerus and Porex shall negotiate in a timely, good faith manner to resolve billing queries. Cerus will not have responsibility to pay for identified errors, incomplete or inaccurate items (collectively the "invoice errors") which result in increases to previously invoiced amounts if the aforementioned invoice errors are communicated to Cerus later than [*] days from the original invoice date.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Payment information' to 'Remuneration details' introduces ambiguity by broadening the scope beyond just monetary payments. The original text and contradicted text specify that Porex will invoice Cerus monthly for 'Services performed' and payments should be made against those invoices. 'Remuneration' can include things other than just payment for services. This creates uncertainty as to what must be included on the invoice, contradicting the implied intent to invoice for services rendered. This ambiguity regarding the information required on the invoice directly contradicts the clause that details the payment process for services and creates a direct conflict (Misaligned Terminology leading to in-text contradiction).

perturbation.original_text: Each work order shall set forth the terms and conditions (including specifications (if applicable), delivery and performance schedules, and fees) pursuant to which Porex will perform the Services. Upon acceptance of a work order by Porex (in writing), such work order shall become a "Statement of Work."
perturbation.changed_text: Each work order shall set forth the terms and conditions (including specifications (if applicable), delivery and performance schedules, and prices) pursuant to which Porex will perform the Services. Upon acceptance of a work order by Porex (in writing), such work order shall become a "Scope of Work."
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'fees' to 'prices' and 'Statement of Work' to 'Scope of Work' creates confusion because the document consistently refers to 'Statement of Work' in other sections. It becomes unclear if the terms refer to the same thing or something different. The text defines that any of the work orders after being accepted by Porex are considered a Statement of Work, but in the modification, they are called Scope of work instead, creating confusion on what they should be called.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new section titled "Pricing for Non-Production Related Work Orders from Cerus" is added to Exhibit D as set forth in Schedule 2 attached. Such pricing, upon mutual agreement of the Parties, shall apply to any Statement of Work mutually agreed by the Parties pursuant to Section 3a.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Statement of Work" to "Scope of Work". The contradicted text references "Statement of Work", which under the original definition would include work orders accepted by Porex. With the change, it's unclear if pricing applies to "Scope of Work" (formerly "Statement of Work") as defined in the changed text, creating ambiguity about which work orders are covered by the pricing agreement. This directly contradicts the clear reference to 'Statement of Work' in the contradicted section, leading to potential disputes over pricing application.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of any conflict between the Agreement and a Statement of Work, the Agreement shall control unless the Statement of Work expressly refers to the Parties' intent to alter the terms of the Agreement with respect to that Statement of Work and shall not be construed as an amendment to the terms of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of any conflict between the Agreement and a Service Agreement, the Agreement shall control unless the Service Agreement expressly refers to the Parties' intent to alter the terms of the Agreement with respect to that Service Agreement and shall not be construed as an amendment to the terms of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Statement of Work' to 'Service Agreement' introduces a new term without defining it. This contradicts the rest of the document, where 'Statement of Work' is used. This is confusing because it leads to confusion on what the correct definition is for the work order
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Section 3a shall be added to the Agreement as follows: "3a Non-Production Related Work. From time to time, Cerus may submit to Porex written work orders substantially in the form of Schedule 3 that specify development activities, engineering analysis, part, material and/or fixture design and development, or other services or activities that Cerus requests Porex to perform that are not activities or services that are necessary for the then-current manufacture and production of Components for Cerus under the Agreement (collectively, as defined in each work order, the "Services"). Each work order shall set forth the terms and conditions (including specifications (if applicable), delivery and performance schedules, and fees) pursuant to which Porex will perform the Services. Upon acceptance of a work order by Porex (in writing), such work order shall become a "Statement of Work." If Porex begins to perform services under a work order that has not been formally agreed in writing, then Porex shall bear the costs of any expenses incurred prior to formal written agreement of the Statement of Work. Cerus hereby consents to Porex's use of the Cerus Dedicated Space and Equipment, as specified in a Statement of Work, solely as necessary to perform the Services under that Statement of Work. Such consent shall automatically terminate upon the completion of the applicable Statement of Work and is limited by the terms of the Agreement. In the event of any conflict between the Agreement and a Service Agreement, the Agreement shall control unless the Service Agreement expressly refers to the Parties' intent to alter the terms of the Agreement with respect to that Service Agreement and shall not be construed as an amendment to the terms of the Agreement."
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Statement of Work' with 'Service Agreement,' creating a contradiction because the contradicted text defines 'Statement of Work' within the context of 'work orders' and references 'Schedule 3.' The change introduces a new term ('Service Agreement') that is not defined, directly contradicting the existing definition and usage of 'Statement of Work' in the contradicted section. This ambiguity makes it unclear which agreement ('Statement of Work' or 'Service Agreement') controls in case of conflict and potentially alters the previously defined obligations and entitlements related to 'Statement of Work', specifically regarding 'Cerus Dedicated Space and Equipment'.
